quality.rationale: 通过坐标变换导出径向运动学关系并与标准测地线方程对比，需串联能量守恒与自由落体判据

origin.question: 15.17. In the preceding problem (15.16) show that the set of coordinate-stationary observers are all in free fall and have zero energy (i.e. they fell in from infinity with zero initial velocity).
origin.answer: Coordinate-stationary observers satisfy $a^{\mu}=0$ and possess unit energy-to-rest-mass ratio at infinity ($E/m=1$), confirming they are in free fall with zero initial velocity.
origin.solution: Solution 15.17. A coordinate-stationary observer in Problem 15.16 satisfies $z =$ constant (see solution to 15.16). By Equation (5) of Solution 15.16, this implies$$\mathrm {d t} = - \mathrm {F}^{\prime} \mathrm {d r} = - \left(\frac {\mathrm {r}}{2 \mathrm {M}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{- 1} \mathrm {d r} \tag {1}$$according to Equation (7) of 15.16. From this equation we get$$\left(\mathrm {d r} / \mathrm {d t}\right)^{2} = (2 \mathrm {M} / \mathrm {r}) \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {M}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{2}. \tag {2}$$Comparison of Equation (2) with Equation (3) of Solution 15.3 (with $\mathfrak{u}_0 = 1$ for a particle falling from rest at infinity) shows that Equation (2) represents a radially falling particle, with unit energy-to-rest-mass ratio at infinity.
